title: rapanea perforata var. typica
intro: Rapanea perforata var. typica, also known as the New Zealand laurel or kawakawa, is a shrub native to New Zealand and eastern Australia. This plant is a member of the Myrsinaceae family and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Rapanea perforata var. typica has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and aiding in wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves can also be smoked or used in topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
